steps.title: Identify the turning-point signal
steps.content: The passage opens by establishing what Nixon is most famous for — Watergate, a scandal. The word But followed by “Nixon's legacy is complex:” signals a pivot to additional facts that challenge the completeness of that singular focus.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B describes this exact pattern: a particular perception is presented first (Nixon famous for Watergate), then additional facts are offered to show that perception is an oversimplification.
  • A describes an achievement followed by reasons for disregard
  • C presents multiple figures for significance comparison
  • D balances evidence both supporting and opposing a claim
.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
